strengths: Successfully completed the circle cutting task within time limit
Maintained good traction control throughout most of the cutting sequence
Demonstrated proper scissor positioning and cutting technique
weaknesses: Minor line deviation penalty indicates precision needs improvement
Completion time of 85 seconds suggests room for efficiency gains
Inconsistent adherence to the marked cutting path

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between sections to verify alignment with the marked line.
drills.targets_weakness: Minor line deviation penalty indicates precision needs improvement

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting geometry throughout the circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent adherence to the marked cutting path

drills.name: Economy-of-motion ladder
drills.description: Perform the circle cutting task slowly with minimal wasted regrasping and repositioning to build muscle memory for efficient movements.
drills.targets_weakness: Completion time of 85 seconds suggests room for efficiency gains

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion ability into faster, more precise cuts while maintaining the quality you've demonstrated.
